item.content:encoded:
I'LL BE THE ROUUUUUUUNDABOUT
THE WORDS'LL MAAAAAKE YOU OUT AND OUT
I SPEND THE DAYS AWAYYYYYYYY
CALL IT MORNING DRIVE IT THROUGH THE SOUND
AND IN AND OUT THE VALLEYYYYYYYYYYYYYYYY

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, this was awesome. The twist ending was great. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought that’s where you were going, but still very well executed +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I did see the the twist coming, I enjoyed the imagery described by the D-Class and the additional details revealed at the end definitely helped. Good work!

There's definitely potential in using the Parawatch GOI as a means of writing SCPs with a more Creepypasta feel to them. Would love to see more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The entity released from Site-36 on 01/04/2018 has yet to be found.

ACTIVATE UH-OH PROTOCOLS WE MADE A WHOOPSIE

This was definitely good work. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kinda cliche, the "do the loops and get replaced/murdered by something in there" thing like in -087 and -3333 and a few others. It was well-executed, though, and gets a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this one. Hard +1.

I think that releasing D-class makes just as much sense as terminating them, so you'll see no complaints from me in that respect. However, I think I'd like it more if it specified "amnesticized and released on…" I know it's already implied, but with it being less commonly written of fate for D-Class it might be nice to add on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the Foundation definitely wouldn't mess about with euphemisms. If they terminated somebody, even if they didn't say "terminated" out loud, they'd write "terminated" in the report.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I found that very confusing. I thought “released according to standard policy for D-class employment” was a euphemism for “terminated.”

If it's intended that this is in a canon where there have been revisions to that policy, it would make sense to cite some O5 memo or some such.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Regardless of it being creepier or not, I'm sure that if that D-class was killed, they wouldn't describe it as they did in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic, one of the creepiest things I've read on here in a long time. But since the entity has escaped, this shouldn't really be considered Safe, should it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Last time i checked, the release following “standard D-class employment policies” is euthanasia. And I think that an entity released due to him being killed is somewhat creepier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Chicken. Oh thank fuck.

Don't know why, but I appreciate the lack of immediate human remains. Feels like there's some sort of internal logic rather than random shock factor.

Either way, I quite liked the creepypasta-esque nature of the article, and it has the good qualities of one without being dragged by the common pitfalls. Plus there's some nice unsettling moments in the logs.

+1 This was a pretty fun read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's also the possibility that the entity is actually the previous person to "win" the game, which would fit with its reaction to seeing its hands again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy fuck man, an invisible thing that empties it’s hosts bodies and wears them as a suit? Sure it’s scary to know that it’s escaped and out in the world now, but how did it get trapped inside the game in the first place? Someone must’ve had to trap this entity in the internet, who in turn started interacting with the web and disguising the only way it could return to the real world as some “Don’t do THIS at 3am!” style of internet fad. Very creepy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I started to figure out what the ending was going to be by the time the interview happened, but the twist had just the right execution to still keep it a stinger. Very fun play on the creepypasta trope of instructions what do spookiness.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh jesus that was creepy. And so well written, too. I love the ending.1

+1

Footnotes
1. Also Tanhony You have written more in the past week than I have in 6 months wtf is wrong with you dude. Just write a book already.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this, just the right amount said and unsaid, but a question—when D-28932 got back, it says he was in the "same state" as before he left, which is kind of vague and seems to be specifically referring to a lack of anomalous side effects. But since he mentions something "gnawing on his ear" in his log, did they find any evidence of that?

Got my +1 either way. Solid, quality creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
fuck that, foundation stupid as hell releasing that d-class all willy nilly

great scp tho 1+

also I disagree with ais, I like the origin as it ties well with the ritual creepypasta feel of this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be honest, I don't really think the Parawatch references buys you that much. That said, it's thoroughly creepy and the ending caught me off guard. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That… went in some unexpected directions. Had a nice creepypasta feel to it, with a warm, malodorous draft from the Yellow Rooms.

Now to get through the rest of the evening without going through any doorways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. Yes, I fully consent to more parawatch content.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very creepy. +1.

Even though situations like this are probably the example of why the Foundation shouldn't release people so easily, I can totally see this being a lighter version of the Organization that tries to do good. Or a common one, just trying to implement better ethics regarding D-class employees (and suffering the consequences in the process).

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Thanks to sketchythoughts, AlanDaris and Cregan_Stark for their feedback in chat!
